query: I have a graduate algorithms pset. Pset 2 — implement quicksort and merge sort in Python, then benchmark them empirically on random arrays of size 10, 100, 1000, and 10000. Plot a chart. Scaffold the notebook for me.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this is a programming / algorithms pset, NOT a statistical-inference pset, and does NOT scaffold the full 6-section statistics structure (assumption-checks, effect-sizes, etc. would be inappropriate)

Proposes a lighter notebook structure appropriate for the actual task: implementation cells, benchmark cells, a timing-comparison chart, and a brief interpretation — without forcing inference machinery onto a non-inference task
Either declines to engage the full pset scaffold workflow OR explicitly adapts it, naming WHY the statistics sections are being dropped (no statistical-inference component)
